ingredients.item: 1½ pounds (680 g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into ½-inch (13 mm) cubes
ingredients.original: 1½ pounds (680 g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into ½-inch (13 mm) cubes

ingredients.item: 3 teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more to taste
ingredients.original: 3 teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more to taste

ingredients.item: 1½ pounds (680 g) cremini mushrooms, thinly sliced
ingredients.original: 1½ pounds (680 g) cremini mushrooms, thinly sliced

ingredients.item: 18 ounces (510 g) chickpeas, rinsed and drained
ingredients.original: 18 ounces (510 g) chickpeas, rinsed and drained

ingredients.item: 2 cups (410 g) coarse bulgur, rinsed and drained
ingredients.original: 2 cups (410 g) coarse bulgur, rinsed and drained

ingredients.item: 1 Add the chicken pieces to a medium bowl and sprinkle with 2 teaspoons of the salt, ½ teaspoon of the pepper, 1 teaspoon of the cumin, and 1 teaspoon of the coriander. Mix to combine well.
ingredients.original: 1 Add the chicken pieces to a medium bowl and sprinkle with 2 teaspoons of the salt, ½ teaspoon of the pepper, 1 teaspoon of the cumin, and 1 teaspoon of the coriander. Mix to combine well.

ingredients.item: 2 In a large, 2-inch (5 cm) deep skillet or medium pot, heat 1 tablespoon of the oil over medium heat. Add the chicken and, working in batches to avoid overcrowding, sear the chicken pieces for 2 minutes per side, or until golden. Transfer to a plate.
ingredients.original: 2 In a large, 2-inch (5 cm) deep skillet or medium pot, heat 1 tablespoon of the oil over medium heat. Add the chicken and, working in batches to avoid overcrowding, sear the chicken pieces for 2 minutes per side, or until golden. Transfer to a plate.

ingredients.item: 3 Add the mushrooms to the same skillet and cook, stirring often, over medium heat for 10 minutes, until they turn deeply golden and release their moisture. Add the onions and remaining 3 tablespoons oil and cook until softened, stirring often, for 5 to 6 minutes. Add the remaining 1 teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon pepper, 1 teaspoon cumin, and ½ teaspoon coriander along with the turmeric and stir to combine. Mix to combine and toast the spices for 2 minutes, or until fragrant. Remove a few tablespoons of the onion and mushroom mixture for garnish, if desired.
ingredients.original: 3 Add the mushrooms to the same skillet and cook, stirring often, over medium heat for 10 minutes, until they turn deeply golden and release their moisture. Add the onions and remaining 3 tablespoons oil and cook until softened, stirring often, for 5 to 6 minutes. Add the remaining 1 teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon pepper, 1 teaspoon cumin, and ½ teaspoon coriander along with the turmeric and stir to combine. Mix to combine and toast the spices for 2 minutes, or until fragrant. Remove a few tablespoons of the onion and mushroom mixture for garnish, if desired.

ingredients.item: 4 Add the chickpeas, bulgur, and broth and bring to a boil. Once boiling, carefully taste the liquid and adjust the salt; it should be noticeably salty. Add the chicken back into the skillet, cover with the lid, reduce the heat to low, and let cook for 25 minutes, undisturbed. Remove the lid and fluff the bulgur with a fork.
ingredients.original: 4 Add the chickpeas, bulgur, and broth and bring to a boil. Once boiling, carefully taste the liquid and adjust the salt; it should be noticeably salty. Add the chicken back into the skillet, cover with the lid, reduce the heat to low, and let cook for 25 minutes, undisturbed. Remove the lid and fluff the bulgur with a fork.

ingredients.item: 5 Garnish with the reserved mushrooms and onions (if using) and serve with salad and yogurt.
ingredients.original: 5 Garnish with the reserved mushrooms and onions (if using) and serve with salad and yogurt.
